rules.description: Caps Lock dual-purpose: tap=Escape, hold+key=text shortcuts (Svorak layout)

manipulators.description: Caps+4 → Paste clipboard with lines joined (for clean prose)

to.shell_command: bash -c 'pbpaste | tr "\n" " " | tr -s " " | pbcopy' && osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down'

manipulators.description: Caps+2 → Quote selection (copies, wraps in <quote> tags)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "c" using command down' -e 'delay 0.2' -e 'set clipContent to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "<quote>" & return & clipContent & return & "</quote>" & return & return' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down'

manipulators.description: Caps+3 → Resume from selection (copies, wraps in <before> tags)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "c" using command down' -e 'delay 0.2' -e 'set clipContent to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "<before>" & return & clipContent & return & "</before>" & return & return & "Proceed. "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down'

manipulators.description: Caps+Enter → Shift+Enter (safety: prevent accidental submit)
Caps+u → Unclear/examine assumptions (Svorak u = QWERTY f)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e "set the clipboard to \"What's unclear or not obvious about this topic/situation/idea? Critically examine our assumptions and rank them from weakest to strongest, according to the evidence at hand, before proceeding. \"" -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+c → Read relevant docs (Svorak c = QWERTY i)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "Read the most relevant 2-3 ones. "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+p → Proceed (Svorak p = QWERTY r)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "Proceed. "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+y → Challenge assumptions (Svorak y = QWERTY t)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "Why are we going down this road? Examine and challenge our crucial assumptions - list them out first and then attack them with critical thinking. "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+s → Sit rep (Svorak s = QWERTY semicolon)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "Sit rep. "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+g → Git state (Svorak g = QWERTY u)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e "set the clipboard to \"What's the git state? \"" -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+d → Doc coherence check (Svorak d = QWERTY h)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "Make sure key docs are up to date and congruent with our findings/progress/reasoning/decisions/ideas. Remember the documentation philosophy/strategy. Be thorough. "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+e → Assess and diagnose, no changes (Svorak e = QWERTY d)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e "set the clipboard to \"Just curious - only assess, explore and diagnose - don't make changes until you have my approval. \"" -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+a → Delegate to subagent (Svorak a = QWERTY a)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "Put a subagent on researching this thoroughly. Report back with findings. "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+x → Git workflow finisher (Svorak x = QWERTY b)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "Commit everything in (chrono)logical groups. Then push. "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+r → Enumerate docs (Svorak r = QWERTY o)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "List 30 recent .md docs related to this work/topic. "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+n → Plan progress check (Svorak n = QWERTY l)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e "set the clipboard to \"What's next according to the plan? \"" -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+t → Run tests (Svorak t = QWERTY k)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "Run the tests. "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+ö → Research that more (Svorak ö = QWERTY e)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e "set the clipboard to \"Super interesting, research that a bit more? Just do like a bunch of google searches yourself, that'd be enough. \"" -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+w → Wait/Hold (Svorak w = QWERTY comma)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e "set the clipboard to \"Don't change or act on anything before I've made any important decisions that need to be made about where to go. \"" -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+å → Summarize (Svorak å = QWERTY q)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "Summarize briefly. "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+f → Fix this (Svorak f = QWERTY y)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "Fix this. "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+h → Clarity / load-bearing idea (Svorak h = QWERTY j)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e "set the clipboard to \"Help me see this clearly: what's the load-bearing idea/s? One bridging analogy. Competing perspectives if relevant. And after you have answered that: what doesn't fit neatly into that view? \"" -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+j → Document insights (Svorak j = QWERTY c)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "Document insights where appropriate. "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+m → /recall (Svorak m = QWERTY m)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "/recall "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+k → /capture (Svorak k = QWERTY v)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "/capture "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+i → /significance (Svorak i = QWERTY g)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "/significance "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+b → /debug-session (Svorak b = QWERTY n)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "/debug-session "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+l → /ui-prototype (Svorak l = QWERTY p)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "/ui-prototype "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+o → /consensus-consult (Svorak o = QWERTY s)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "/consensus-consult "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+q → /docs-review (Svorak q = QWERTY x)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "/docs-review "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+ä → Zoom out / open loops check (Svorak ä = QWERTY w)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "Let'\''s zoom out. How did we get here? What were we in the middle of? Are there any open loops we forgot about? "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+. → Pull and push (Svorak . = QWERTY z)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "Pull and push. "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+v → Research extensively (Svorak v = QWERTY period)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "Research this extensively online. "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+z → /total-recap (Svorak z = QWERTY slash)

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "/total-recap "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+6 → Persist session knowledge to disk

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "I have found a lot of the reasoning and research insights and angles from this session to be highly valuable. Make sure that everything from this session — including all pedagogical examples and reasoning from both you and me — is pretty much verbatim persisted to disk in one of our documents, or in whatever document(s) is most appropriate. "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+7 → Synthesize insights to overview docs

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "Please mirror individual high-level crucial insights briefly into whichever higher level overview doc seems most appropriate and relevant to you, and have each insight reference the doc where they are explored more at length. "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+8 → Autonomous deep parallel research

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e "set the clipboard to \"Do some deep parallel sub-agent research on your own favorite angles on what we've been working on. Feel free to look into closely related areas and any new creative angles you can think of. Make sure all research is persisted to disk afterwards. \"" -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+9 → Adversarial research / poke holes

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "Run a few more parallel sub-agent research threads to investigate and inform further synthesis between the various conceptual threads that we have covered thus far, and try to poke holes in this vision and reflect deeply. "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+0 → Acknowledge aspect needs design

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e "set the clipboard to \"Interesting, that's certainly one aspect that has to be solved / consciously designed. \"" -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+1 → README structure constraint

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "Make the README maximum two paragraphs and the rest of the readme should be a table of contents including all documents. "' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'

manipulators.description: Caps+5 → Ultrathink

to.shell_command: osascript -e 'set prev to the clipboard' -e 'set the clipboard to "Ultrathink"' -e 'tell application "System Events" to keystroke "v" using command down' -e 'delay 0.1' -e 'set the clipboard to prev'
